summary: Regression verification after react-day-picker was COMPLETELY REMOVED (dependency uninstalled + src/components/ui/calendar.jsx deleted). All flows still pass: (1) landing page loads with hero headline 'O cuidado dentário deve ser tão confortável quanto o resultado.' and hero image visible, no console/page errors; (2) navbar #agendar link smooth-scrolls to appointment section; (3) #agendar and #atmosfera anchors exist; (4) appointment form end-to-end works — name/email/phone filled, treatment selected, native date input accepts '2026-03-15', submit -> form-success + success toast 'Pedido enviado. Entraremos em contacto em breve.'; (5) POST /api/appointments persists data (verified via GET) and returns 'id' (no '_id') with status 'novo'; (6) all 22 items in GET /api/appointments contain 'id' and no '_id'; (7) backend pytest 9/9 passed.
critical_code_review_comments: Confirmed: react-day-picker no longer in package.json/yarn.lock; calendar.jsx deleted. Clean removal, no dead imports.
Carried over (not addressed, low priority): server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown') — migrate to lifespan handler.
Carried over (not addressed, low priority): consider a dedicated AppointmentOut response schema so response_model_by_alias=False isn't needed per-route.
test_credentials: N/A - public landing page, no auth.
seed_data_creation: One appointment added via UI submit (email test_reg_1783161072@example.pt) + pytest TEST_* rows. Safe to purge by email prefix if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: react-day-picker is fully removed (0 refs in yarn.lock/package.json/src). Appointment form uses native <input type='date' data-testid='input-date'>. Backend /api/appointments (POST/GET) both return 'id' and never '_id'. Reuse /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. If running pytest, set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var (test file reads it directly).
rca of the issue: N/A - regression run only, no bugs found.
